ddls:
CREATE TABLE "planets" (
  "planet_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each planet */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the planet */,
  "mass" REAL /* Mass of the planet in Earth masses */,
  "diameter" INTEGER /* Diameter of the planet in kilometers */,
  "day_length" REAL /* Length of a day on the planet in hours */,
  "sun_distance" REAL /* Average distance from the Sun in millions of kilometers */,
  "orbit_period" REAL /* Time it takes the planet to orbit the Sun in days */,
  "orbit_velocity" REAL /* Speed of the planet in its orbit in km/s */,
  "mean_temperature" INTEGER /* Average temperature on the planet in degrees Celsius */,
  "surface_pressure" REAL /* Surface pressure of the planet in atm */,
  "magnetic_field" TEXT /* Presence of a magnetic field (yes/no) */,
  "composition" TEXT /* Chemical composition of the planet */,
  "atmosphere_presence" TEXT /* Presence of an atmosphere (yes/no) */,
  "atmosphere_composition" TEXT /* Chemical composition of the atmosphere */,
  "surface_features" TEXT /* Notable surface features (e.g., mountains, valleys) */,
  "image_urls" TEXT /* URLs of images of the planet */,
  "discovery_date" TEXT /* Date the planet was discovered */,
  "research_papers" TEXT /* References to key research papers about the planet */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("planet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "moons" (
  "moon_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each moon */,
  "planet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the planet the moon orbits */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the moon */,
  "diameter" INTEGER /* Diameter of the moon in kilometers */,
  "orbital_period" REAL /* Orbital period of the moon in days */,
  "mean_temperature" INTEGER /* Average temperature on the moon in degrees Celsius */,
  "surface_pressure" REAL /* Surface pressure of the moon in atm */,
  "image_urls" TEXT /* URLs of images of the moon */,
  "discovery_date" TEXT /* Date the moon was discovered */,
  "research_papers" TEXT /* References to key research papers about the moon */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("moon_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_moons_planet_id FOREIGN KEY ("planet_id") REFERENCES planets ("planet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "rings" (
  "ring_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each ring system */,
  "planet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the planet with the ring system */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the ring system */,
  "width" INTEGER /* Width of the ring system in kilometers */,
  "composition" TEXT /* Chemical composition of the ring system */,
  "thickness" INTEGER /* Thickness of the ring system in kilometers */,
  "image_urls" TEXT /* URLs of images of the ring system */,
  "discovery_date" TEXT /* Date the ring system was discovered */,
  "research_papers" TEXT /* References to key research papers about the ring system */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("ring_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_rings_planet_id FOREIGN KEY ("planet_id") REFERENCES planets ("planet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "missions" (
  "mission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each mission */,
  "planet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the planet visited by the mission */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the mission */,
  "first_visit_date" TEXT /* Date of the first visit to the planet */,
  "mission_type" TEXT /* Type of mission (e.g., flyby, orbiter, lander) */,
  "launch_date" TEXT /* Launch date of the mission */,
  "mission_status" TEXT /* Current status of the mission (e.g., active, completed, failed) */,
  "mission_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the mission in days */,
  "mission_objectives" TEXT /* Primary objectives of the mission */,
  "mission_outcomes" TEXT /* Outcomes of the mission */,
  "mission_images" TEXT /* URLs of images from the mission */,
  "research_papers" TEXT /* References to key research papers about the mission */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("mission_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_missions_planet_id FOREIGN KEY ("planet_id") REFERENCES planets ("planet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, scientist, admin) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the user */,
  "access_level" TEXT /* Access level of the user (e.g., read, write, admin) */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Last login date of the user */,
  "creation_date" TEXT /* Date the user account was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)
